input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-11T01:02:56.314Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The markup extension `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` automatically picks the right value whenever the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

Use `SetAppThemeColor` for colors and `SetAppTheme<T>` for other properties:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Color.FromArgb("#000000"), Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));
//                                               ↑ light value              ↑ dark value

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// For non-color properties:
label.SetAppTheme<string>(Label.TextProperty, "Light Mode", "Dark Mode");
```

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reactivity** | Both approaches respond live to OS theme changes at runtime. |
| **Default** | If `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` is `Unspecified`, the `Light` value is used. |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system. |
| **Styles** | You can use `AppThemeBinding` inside `<Style>` setters to theme globally instead of per-element. |
